title: Logging personal data (PII)
description: Writing personally identifiable information - a person's name, Social Security number, email, phone, postal address, date of birth, or payment-card number - into logs or stdout, e.g. log.info("user " + name + ", ssn=" + ssn). Logs are retained and shared, so PII in logs is a compliance violation (GDPR/CCPA). This is about PERSONAL data, NOT secrets - passwords, tokens, and API keys are covered by sensitive-logging instead. Fix: log a user id or request id rather than the personal data. SAFE: logging a non-personal identifier such as a user id, request id, or status code.

title: Storing PII in cleartext
description: Persisting personally identifiable information (SSN, payment-card number, email, postal address, date of birth) to a file or database with no masking, tokenization, or encryption, e.g. open(path).write(ssn) or an INSERT of a raw card number. Regulated data must be protected at rest. Fix: mask, tokenize, or encrypt the value before storing it. SAFE: storing a hashed, tokenized, or masked value, or storing non-personal data.
